faqs.question: What causes drain fly infestations in Bedford Park homes?
faqs.answer: Drain flies breed in the organic buildup inside drains, pipes, and sewer lines. In Bedford Park's older buildings, aging plumbing and insufficient drain maintenance create ideal breeding conditions. They're commonly found in kitchen sinks, bathroom drains, basement floor drains, and any area with standing water or organic residue.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take in Bedford Park?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments in Bedford Park take 1-2 hours depending on the severity of the infestation and number of affected drains. Our technicians thoroughly treat all breeding sites, apply bio-enzymatic cleaners, and may use foaming agents to reach deep into pipes. Follow-up visits may be recommended for severe infestations.

faqs.question: Is drain fly treatment safe for my Bedford Park apartment or business?
faqs.answer: Yes, our drain fly treatments use EPA-approved products that are safe for residential and commercial properties. We employ eco-friendly bio-enzymatic cleaners that break down organic matter without harsh chemicals. These treatments are safe around children, pets, and food preparation areas when applied by our licensed professionals.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350–$550 drain fly treatment service?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a thorough inspection of all drains and potential breeding sites, treatment of affected drains with professional-grade bio-enzymatic cleaners and foaming agents, mechanical cleaning when necessary, identification of plumbing issues, and preventative recommendations. The price varies based on property size, infestation severity, and number of drains requiring treatment.
